summary: Cycle 2026-03-30T12:10Z. KE-8 sprint complete. PR #1448 (fix: claude-hook allow-path must not write to stderr) was 5 commits behind main — rebased by kernel EM, CI re-triggered (4/4 green), merged. Closes #1430 (ageRuns was 2, director escalation cleared). PR #1453 (docs: sync invariant count to 26 across all documentation) also merged — CI was 4/4 green from earlier run. New P0 swarm health alert #1452 (69.5% failure rate, zombie vitest processes, stuck worktrees) is infra/human scope — no kernel action available. Security issue #1449 (path-to-regexp ReDoS HIGH, brace-expansion MEDIUM) triaged as P2 kernel maintenance for KE-9. #1177 (github.pr.* policy gap) confirmed CLOSED. Issue #1431 (codex budget) remains infra scope.

issues.title: [dogfood] git commit Bash tool calls blocked by hook even when decision is 'allowed'
issues.note: Rule: only denials use stderr. Allow paths produce zero stderr. 3 new tests added (git commit, git push, gh CLI allowed with no policy loaded — all assert process.stderr.write never called). Rebase by kernel EM (5 commits behind main, state.json conflict resolved by keeping main's version).

dogfoodObservations.description: FIRST-PARTY: claude-hook PreToolUse output on stderr blocked allowed actions in Claude Code. PR #1448 merged — allow paths now produce zero stderr. Rule enforced via 3 new test assertions.

PROCESS: PR #1448 branch had stale EM/QA state.json commits mixed with the code fix. This caused a rebase conflict when the branch fell 5 commits behind main. Consider having fix PRs contain only the code change — separate state management from fix commits.
0 governance denials this cycle. No LOCKDOWN triggers. All governed operations clean.
